His work explores how electrons behave in extremely small structures, contributing foundational understanding to spintronics, molecular electronics, and superconducting device physics.

department: Elmore Family School of Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: The lab focuses on AI/ML systems, sustainable computing, computer systems, computer architecture, and machine learning with healthcare applications. Research develops efficient and sustainable computing infrastructure for AI workloads.
professor_about: Yi Ding is an Assistant Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Purdue University. He completed postdoctoral work at MIT in 2023 and earned his PhD from the University of Chicago in 2020. His research spans the intersection of artificial intelligence systems, sustainable computing, and healthcare applications, addressing computer architecture challenges for modern ML workloads.

department: Elmore Family School of Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: The lab focuses on computational imaging, computer vision, machine learning, and optics. Research develops computational methods for imaging and vision applications, leveraging machine learning and optical principles to advance signal and image processing.
professor_about: Qi Guo is an Assistant Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Purdue University. His research centers on developing computational methods for imaging and vision applications. He combines machine learning, optics, and signal processing to create novel imaging systems and algorithms for challenging visual computing problems.

department: Elmore Family School of Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: The lab focuses on device-circuit co-design in emerging nanotechnologies for Boolean and non-Boolean computing. Research encompasses spintronics, low power variation tolerant VLSI design, and device-circuit modeling for next-generation computing architectures.
professor_about: Sumeet Gupta is the Elmore Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Purdue University. He earned his PhD from Purdue in 2012 and his BTech from IIT Delhi. His research bridges emerging nanotechnologies with circuit design, developing novel computing paradigms using spintronics and other non-traditional device technologies for energy-efficient computation.

department: Elmore Family School of Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: The lab investigates the intersection of control theory and machine learning, distributed estimation and decision making, and cyber-physical-human systems. Research encompasses signal processing, game theory, and applications to power grids and transportation networks.
professor_about: Vijay Gupta is the Elmore Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Associate Head of Graduate and Professional Programs at Purdue University. His research bridges control systems and AI methodologies with emphasis on distributed computational approaches in complex networked environments. His work spans infrastructure systems including electrical grids and transportation networks alongside human-centered cyber-physical systems.

department: Elmore Family School of Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: The lab focuses on hybrid systems, multi-agent systems, control of uncertain systems, and control applications in sensor networks and energy efficient building management. Research addresses general control and optimization theory with practical system applications.
professor_about: Jianghai Hu is a Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Purdue University. He earned his PhD from the University of California at Berkeley in 2003. His research spans hybrid systems, multi-agent coordination, and control applications from sensor networks to energy-efficient building management, combining theoretical advances in control and optimization with practical system implementations.

department: Elmore Family School of Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: The lab focuses on distributed systems, operating systems, networking, and parallel computing. Research addresses fundamental challenges in building efficient and scalable computing infrastructure across distributed environments.
professor_about: Y. Charlie Hu is the Michael and Katherine Birck Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Purdue University. He earned his PhD in Computer Science from Harvard University in 1997 and holds degrees from Yale University and the University of Science and Technology of China. His research spans distributed systems, operating systems, networking, and parallel computing.

description: The lab encompasses analysis of algorithms, information theory, bioinformatics, analytic combinatorics, and stability problems of distributed systems. Research includes directing an NSF Science and Technology Center focused on information science.
professor_about: Wojciech Szpankowski is the Saul Rosen Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at Purdue University with a courtesy appointment in Electrical and Computer Engineering. He is an IEEE Fellow, Erskine Fellow, and recipient of the 2010 Humboldt Research Award. He founded the Institute for Science of Information in 2008 and directs an NSF Science and Technology Center, with authored monographs on average case algorithm analysis.
